ogTitle: Felixstow vs Ferryden Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Felixstow and Ferryden Park.
heading: Felixstow vs Ferryden Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Felixstow, South Australia and Ferryden Park, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Felixstow has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Ferryden Park (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Felixstow or Ferryden Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Felixstow has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Ferryden Park scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Felixstow scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Ferryden Park scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Felixstow or Ferryden Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Felixstow has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
